status_notes: Initial research from Azure Front Door documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs). Needs Pass 2 (API), Pass 3 (cross-reference), and Pass 5 (human review with live Azure environment).
notes: Global load balancer, CDN, and WAF. Security includes DDoS protection, WAF policies, private link to origins, custom domains with managed TLS.

match_nodes.description: Web Application Firewall with managed and custom rules

data.description: Web Application Firewall integrated with Front Door. Supports DRS (Default Rule Set) managed rules based on OWASP CRS, custom rules for specific patterns, bot protection, and rate limiting. Two modes: Detection (log only) and Prevention (block).

options.description: DRS (Default Rule Set) based on OWASP Core Rule Set. Covers SQLi, XSS, LFI, RFI, command injection, protocol violations.
User-defined rules matching on IP, geo, request size, headers, body, cookies. Priority-based evaluation.
Bot Manager rule set identifies good bots, bad bots, and unknown bots.
Rate limit custom rules to throttle by IP, geo, or other attributes.
Detection logs matches; Prevention blocks matches.

data.vendor_recommendation: Start in Detection mode, review logs, tune rules to reduce false positives, then switch to Prevention mode. Use DRS 2.1 for the latest rule coverage. Enable bot protection for public-facing applications.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Front Door → WAF policies

configure.cli: az network front-door waf-policy create --name {policy} --resource-group {rg} --mode Prevention
configure.cli_managed_rule: az network front-door waf-policy managed-rules add --policy-name {policy} --resource-group {rg} --type DefaultRuleSet --version 2.1

data.description: Azure Front Door provides built-in DDoS protection at the edge. All Front Door profiles include infrastructure-level DDoS protection (Azure DDoS Infrastructure Protection) at no additional cost. Absorbs volumetric attacks at the edge before they reach origins.

default.note: DDoS infrastructure protection is automatically enabled for all Front Door profiles. No configuration required.

data.vendor_recommendation: Front Door's built-in DDoS protection handles volumetric attacks. For additional protection (adaptive tuning, attack analytics, cost protection), enable Azure DDoS Network Protection on the VNet containing your origins.

audit.note: DDoS infrastructure protection is always enabled and requires no configuration. Check Azure DDoS Network Protection status on origin VNets for enhanced protection.

match_nodes.description: Connect to backend origins via Private Link

data.description: Connect Front Door Premium to backend origins via Azure Private Link. Origins do not need public IP addresses. Traffic from Front Door to the origin travels over the Microsoft backbone network. Supports App Service, Storage, Application Gateway, and any Private Link service.

options.description: Connect to web apps without public access
Connect to storage accounts without public endpoints
Connect to any internal service behind a Private Link service

data.vendor_recommendation: Use Private Link origins to eliminate public exposure of backend services. Disable public access on origins after configuring Private Link. Requires Front Door Premium tier.

audit.note: Non-null sharedPrivateLinkResource indicates Private Link is configured.
audit.console: Azure Portal → Front Door → Origin groups → {Origin} → Private link

configure.cli: az afd origin create --resource-group {rg} --profile-name {profile} --origin-group-name {group} --origin-name {name} --host-name {origin-host} --origin-host-header {origin-host} --shared-private-link-resource private-link-location={region} groupId={group-id} privateLinkId={resource-id}
configure.note: Private Link connection must be approved on the origin resource after creation.

data.description: Front Door provides automatic HTTPS for custom domains with managed certificates. Front Door managed certificates are free, auto-provisioned, and auto-renewed. Custom certificates from Azure Key Vault are also supported for organizations requiring specific CA or extended validation.

options.description: Free, auto-provisioned, auto-renewed by DigiCert. Domain validation via CNAME.
Bring your own certificate stored in Azure Key Vault. Supports specific CA requirements and EV certificates.

default.note: Front Door managed certificates are the default for custom domains.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use Front Door managed certificates for simplicity and automatic renewal. Use Key Vault certificates only when specific CA, EV, or wildcard requirements exist. Ensure Key Vault access policies allow Front Door to read certificates.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Front Door → Custom domains → {Domain} → Certificate type

match_nodes.description: Minimum TLS version for client connections

data.description: Configure the minimum TLS protocol version that Front Door accepts for client connections. Supports TLS 1.0, 1.2, and 1.3. TLS 1.2 is recommended as the minimum for security compliance.

options.description: Legacy. Only for backward compatibility with very old clients.
Recommended minimum. Required by most compliance frameworks (PCI DSS, HIPAA).
Latest version. Best security and performance.

default.note: TLS 1.2 is the default minimum version for new Front Door profiles.

data.vendor_recommendation: Set minimum TLS version to 1.2 or higher. TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are considered insecure. Monitor access logs for clients using older TLS versions before removing support.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Front Door → Custom domains → {Domain} → Minimum TLS version

match_nodes.description: IP-based and geo-based filtering via WAF custom rules

data.description: Restrict access based on client IP address or geographic location using WAF custom rules. IP-based rules match against specific addresses or CIDR ranges. Geo-based rules match against country/region codes. Can be combined with other match conditions.

options.description: Match on RemoteAddr (client IP) or SocketAddr (TCP connection IP). Allow or block specific IPs or ranges.
Match on GeoLocation country code. Block or allow traffic from specific countries.
Action to take on matched requests

data.vendor_recommendation: Use geo-filtering for compliance with data sovereignty requirements. Use IP filtering to restrict access to known corporate or partner IP ranges. Combine both for defense in depth.

audit.note: Check custom rules for MatchConditions with MatchVariable RemoteAddr or GeoLocation.
audit.console: Azure Portal → Front Door → WAF policies → {Policy} → Custom rules

configure.cli_geo: az network front-door waf-policy rule create --policy-name {policy} --resource-group {rg} --name BlockCountry --priority 100 --action Block --rule-type MatchRule --match-variable RemoteAddr --operator GeoMatch --values CN RU
configure.cli_ip: az network front-door waf-policy rule create --policy-name {policy} --resource-group {rg} --name AllowCorporate --priority 50 --action Allow --rule-type MatchRule --match-variable RemoteAddr --operator IPMatch --values 203.0.113.0/24
